title: n-PCR for 10 samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7ihd1","text":"HTLV-1 diagnosis using PCR is based on the amplification of the viral DNA sequences. This is usually conducted using a nested PCR which involves two rounds of DNA amplification reactions targeting the viral polymerase gene. In this case, we set up the conditions to analyze a pool of 10 samples in a single n-PCR detecting the presence of HTLV-1.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"cutlj","text":"To avoid contamination risk, DNA extraction, Pre PCR set-up (mastermix preparation), and post-PCR procedures should be performed at least in  separate workplaces.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cpa3p","text":"Benches, work stations, centrifuges, vortexes, and pipettes must be cleaned  with 70% ethanol before and after every PCR set-up.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ff5kd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3i68o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4i4e9","text":"Gloves should be changed regularly, particularly after preparation and before the dispensing of master mixes into PCR plates or strips to avoid cross contamination.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a10ig","text":"Be aware that virus genetic material is being use, so be carful.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Equipment and Materials Pipettes 10µl presterilized filter tips 20µl presterilized filter tips 200µl presterilized filter tips 1000µl presterilized filter tips Thermal Cycle (we used TProfessional TRIO) Electrophoresis equipment

Reagents and Chemicals Taq DNA Polymerase kit (1000 U), by Qiagen , Catalog #: Cat No./ID: 201205 Buffers (PCR Buffer and CoralLoad PCR Buffer) MgCl2 Q solution Taq polymerase dNTPs Nuclease free H2O Forward (F) and reverse (R) primers Agarose Markers: 50pb and/or 100pb (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Germany)

PCR summary protocol Primary PCR/ Nest-1 PCR should be done using PolEF and PolER primers. For each group of samples include positive control (DNA from MT2 cell line) and a negative control of PCR mix without a DNA template. Nest-2 PCR should be done using the primers PolIF and PolIR. Also the primers Actin F and Actin R are necessary to amplify human Actin gene. Once the PCR run is complete, remove tubes and briefly centrifuge to spin down products. Store PCR tubes in the fridge or freezer Run the samples on 2% Agarose gel electrophoresis using gel red nucleic acid stain. Score results as presence or absence of bands comparing with control bands.

PCR Protocol For each run, at least two controls (1 positive and 1 negative) should be included in addition to the samples. Prepare the PCR master mix calculating enough volume for number of the samples +8%, to account for pipetting loss and dead volume. Master Mix for Nest-1 PCR ( 10X PCR buffer , dNTPs, MgCl2, primers, Taq polymerase and water) Master Mix for Nest-2 PCR ( 10X CoralLoad PCR Buffer , dNTPs, MgCl2, primers, Taq polymerase and water)
Preparation of PCR (Nest-1 PCR) x 10 %s %s Preparation of PCR (Nest-2 PCR) x 10 %s %s
PCR Cycling conditions Nest-1 : Pol EF + Pol ER %s %s Nest-2 : Pol IF + Pol IR + Act F + Acr R %s %s

Preparation and storage of reagents Stock reagents for PCR (10X buffer, dNTPs, MgCl2, Q solution and primers) and enzymes should be stored at -20°C. Stock reagents should be diluted into working concentrations and aliquoted at -20°C into separate tubes. Working aliquots of sample and control DNA as well as primary PCR (Nest-1) products must be kept at -20°C for long-term storage but can be kept in the fridge for daily use.
creator: Matias Ruggieri
